comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Implemented in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py: pass3_decide() + validity()/impact()/severity_label(). Pass-3 is fully deterministic (no model in the decision path): validity = graded fraction of the binary sub-answers (yes=1/insufficient=.5/no=0); impact = mean of the ordinal-mapped severity attributes; unified priority = validity x impact; decision = block|advisory|dropped with the cited-reference veto + per-criterion block_threshold (default 0.95 => advisory in v1). INDETERMINATE when no verification. Wired into orchestrator._run_passes (Pass-3 loop). Unit-tested: tests/unit/test_plan_review.py::test_pass3_* (validity, impact/priority, advisory-by-default, opt-in blocking, low-validity drop, cited-ref veto, indeterminate).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification: PASS

All five acceptance criteria are met by the implementation in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` and `orchestrator.py`.

**AC1 — Deterministic veto → confidence → severity → decision; no model in path.**
`pass3_decide()` (passes.py:390–444) is pure arithmetic. Cited-reference veto fires at line 422 (`cited_reference_accurate == "no"` → dropped), validity is a graded fraction (line 360–367), impact is an ordinal mean (line 370–377), priority = val × imp (line 420), decision by conditionals. No model call anywhere in the decision path.

**AC2 — Advisory-only default; per-criterion blocking opt-in; thresholds configurable.**
`blocking_enabled=False` is the default parameter (passes.py:394). The orchestrator reads `default_posture` per criterion (orchestrator.py:438–441). `block_threshold` defaults to 0.95 (passes.py:52) and is read per criterion from the registry (orchestrator.py:435).

**AC3 — Unified priority score (deterministic: validity × impact) drives block-threshold + advisory ranking.**
`priority = round(val * imp, 4)` (passes.py:420); advisory sorted by `-priority` (orchestrator.py:333); block check uses `priority >= block_threshold` (passes.py:433). No model produces any score.

**AC4 — Advisory surfacing cap = config top-N (default 10); overflow → sidecar; BLOCKING never capped.**
`DEFAULT_ADVISORY_CAP = 10` (orchestrator.py:41); `surfaced = advisory[:advisory_cap]` / `overflow = advisory[advisory_cap:]` (orchestrator.py:334–335); blocking list is never touched by the cap; an assertion guards against blocking findings leaking into advisory (lines 330–332); `advisory_cap` is a tunable parameter on the public API (`__init__.py:44,60,61`).

**AC5 — Unit-tested aggregation (veto, confidence, severity buckets, decision).**
Seven `test_pass3_*` functions in `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py` (lines 124–159) cover: graded fraction/validity, impact+priority+severity, default advisory, opt-in blocking, low-validity drop, cited-reference veto, and indeterminate.

**Verdict: PASS** — every requirement is demonstrably satisfied by the implementation.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The model emits no holistic severity/confidence anywhere in the decision path; the decision is deterministic
code.

## What
- Cited-reference veto (ONLY when a code citation is present) -> a UNIFIED PRIORITY SCORE computed DETERMINISTICALLY
  from the Pass-2 sub-answers. FORMULA (v1 default — per the epic's Pass-3 spec; the attribute weights are
  calibration-tunable): validity ∈ [0,1] = graded fraction of the binary sub-answers (yes=1 / insufficient=.5 /
  no=0); impact ∈ [0,1] = the deterministic mean of the five Pass-2 severity attributes, each ordinal mapped to
  [0,1] (prod/debt_impact max; blast_radius; likelihood; reversibility); PRIORITY = validity × impact ∈ [0,1]
  (both LLM-judged spectrums, so a low-validity/high-impact finding can outrank a high-validity/low-impact one).
  decision = DROP if validity < .5; else BLOCK iff (not cited-reference-vetoed) AND priority ≥ the criterion's
  `block_threshold` (default 0.95, criterion opted into blocking); else ADVISORY.
- ADVISORY SURFACING CAP: the surfaced advisory findings are the top-N by priority score, N = a CONFIG value
  (default 10, owned by `55de`); advisory overflow beyond N -> `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar (recorded, not surfaced).
  BLOCKING findings are EXEMPT from the cap: never dropped; if blocking count > N, ALL blocking findings are
  returned. The cap can never suppress a block.

- Pass-3's SURVIVING (advisory, not-dropped) findings feed the Pass-4 affirmative coach `75a9` (crusty-herb-stand), which primes the caller with a next move per finding and NEVER alters this pass's decision.
## Scope
- Pure code (no LLM). Defaults advisory-only in v1 (only the DET floor blocks); per-criterion blocking is
  opt-in; thresholds project-overridable and start high.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Deterministic veto -> confidence -> severity -> decision; no model holistic severity/confidence in the path.
- [ ] Advisory-only default; per-criterion blocking opt-in; thresholds configurable.
- [ ] Unified priority score (deterministic: validity + impact combined) drives block-threshold + advisory ranking; no model holistic score in the path.
- [ ] Advisory surfacing cap = config top-N (default 10, owned by `55de`); overflow advisory -> sidecar; BLOCKING never capped (ALL blocking returned even if > N); cap is config-driven + tunable.
- [ ] Unit-tested aggregation (veto, confidence, severity buckets, decision).
compiled_state.title: Pass-3 deterministic aggregator
